question: [File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/config/GraphQlConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: graphQlCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a query with complexity exactly 200 (at the MaxQueryComplexityInstrumentation limit) using fragment spreads that are counted differently by the complexity calculator, causing the instrumentation to undercount complexity and allow execution of a query that exceeds the intended resource budget?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/config/GraphQlConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: graphQlCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged external user use inline fragments in a query to cause MaxQueryComplexityInstrumentation(200) to miscalculate complexity (since inline fragments may not add to the complexity score), effectively executing a query with true complexity far exceeding 200 and exhausting database resources?
